x4freetext: A K-400 neutron generator
The reaction of 3H(d,n)4He was used to provide
neutrons in 13 - 15 MeV with a beam current of about
200 uA and deuteron beam energy of about 134 keV. A
tritium-titanium target with a thickness of 2.65
mg/cm2.
2.3-5.2 g of pure natural
isotopic xenon gas (99.999% pure) at a pressure of
120-250 atm in a 1 mm thick stainless steel sphere
container with an inner diameter of 20 mm were used
for our experiments. Five samples and ten diameter 20
mm monitors (0.12 mm thick niobium foils with a
purity of 99.99%) were prepared for irradiation. Five
stacks of ZrNbAl-Xe-AlNbZr were made and mounted at 0
deg, 45 deg, 90 deg, 110 deg, and 135deg relative to
the direction of the beam. The iron density is 7.86
g/cm3
Sample No.           Samples density (g/cm3)
2 h irradiation at a yield of (4 - 5)E10 n/s.
The distance between the center of the sample
sphere and the end of the neutron production target
was usually set to 5 cm. A cadmium foil (1 mm thick
foil with a purity of 99.95%) was used to wrap the
sample.
An Au-Si surface barrier detector at an angle
135 deg was used to detect particles to correct small
variations in the neutron flux.
The efficiency of HPGe detector (ORTEC, GEM-
60P, USA) with the crystal length, crystal diameter,
energy resolution at 1.332 MeV, and comparative
effectiveness were 72.3 mm, 70.1 mm 1.69 keV, and
about 68%, respectively, was calibrated according to
the 152Eu, 133Ba, 137Cs, and 226Ra standard sources.
F = Fc x Fg x Fs x Fo.
Fc, Fg, Fs, and Fo represent the coefficients
reflecting the coincidence sum impact of cascade
g-rays, sample counting geometry, self-absorption for
the specific gamma-ray energy, and the solid angle of
neutron flux. Fo = 1.0756
Gamma-ray   u/p             u              Correction
energy(keV) Fe     Xe       Fe    No.  Xe    factors
